Ukraine Conflict News





27 December 2025 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • Smith Questions Trump Administration's Strikes in Nigeria House Armed Services Committee - Democrats 27 Dec 2025 -- U.S. Representative Adam Smith (D-Wash.), Ranking Member of the House Armed Services Committee, released the following statement in response to the Trump Administration's recent strikes against the Islamic State in Nigeria.

  • US cannot turn Venezuela into a colony and steal its natural resources, Maduro warns Press TV 27 Dec 2025 -- Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro has warned that the United States cannot turn the sovereign Latin American country into a colony and steal its natural resources.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • The President visited the Joint Force command post President of Russia 27 Dec 2025 -- The Supreme Commander-in-Chief visited one of the command posts of the Joint Force and chaired a meeting with senior officials of the General Staff and commanders of groups of forces.

  • $45 billion from partners, over 3 million strike drones, more Ukrainian weapons: key Ministry of Defence highlights Ukraine MOD 27 Dec 2025
  • MoD approves digital fuel accounting concept: what will change and why it matters Ukraine MOD 27 Dec 2025
  • Prime Minister Carney announces new support for a just and lasting peace in Ukraine Prime Minister of Canada 27 Dec 2025 -- Today, Prime Minister Carney welcomed President Zelenskyy to Halifax, marking the President's second visit to Canada this year. Ukraine is at the frontline of the struggle between democracy and authoritarianism.

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

    Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • The Chief of the General Staff Conducted a Situational Assessment in the Central Command Israel Defense Forces 27 Dec 2025

  • Israeli forces kill Palestinian man in Gaza; settler violence, abductions intensify in West Bank Press TV 27 Dec 2025 -- Israeli forces have fatally shot a Palestinian man in Gaza amid ongoing ceasefire violations, as violence and abductions escalate across the occupied West Bank.

  • Hezbollah rejects internal discord, will not fight Lebanese army, says top official Press TV 27 Dec 2025 -- Head of the Executive Council of Hezbollah, Sheikh Ali Damoush, says the resistance movement will not be drawn into confrontation with the Lebanese Army and will not allow internal discord, emphasizing that any attempts to provoke division in the country are doomed to fail.
  • Israeli strike injures UN peacekeeper in southern Lebanon Press TV 27 Dec 2025 -- The United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) says an Israeli strike in the southern part of the country injured a peacekeeper, reasserting renewed calls for the Tel Aviv regime to "halt aggressive actions" in the region.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Costs Mount As Afghanistan-Pakistan Trade War Strands Thousands Of Trucks RFE/RL 27 Dec 2025 -- For nearly two months, Sayed Wali, a young Afghan truck driver, has watched the sun rise, and the sun set in the fabled Khyber Pass, which connects western Pakistan to eastern Afghanistan. But he's not here for the view. He's stuck.

  • Saudi-backed coalition vows to counter UAE allies' military actions in Yemen Press TV 27 Dec 2025 -- The Saudi-led military coalition says it would respond directly to any military activities by the UAE-allied Southern Transitional Council (STC) that disrupt ongoing de-escalation efforts in southern Yemen.
  • Raging South Yemen proxy war meant to pave way for Israeli dominance, warns Ansarullah Press TV 27 Dec 2025 -- A senior Ansarullah official has censured armed clashes between forces loyal to the Saudi-backed fugitive government and the UAE-allied Southern Transitional Council (STC) militants in southern Yemen, warning that the proxy struggle is cleverly meant to help the Israeli regime dominate the oil-rich region.

  • On 26 December 2025, security forces conducted an intelligence based operation in Panjgur District of Balochistan, on reported presence of Khwarij belonging to Indian proxy, Fitna al Khwarij. ISPR Pakistan 27 Dec 2025

  • Widespread condemnations pour in after deadly Shia mosque attack in Syria IRNA 27 Dec 2025 -- Condemnations have poured in following a terrorist attack on a Shia mosque in the western Syrian city of Homs that killed at least eight people and wounded 18 others.
  • Hezbollah condemns deadly terror attack on worshippers in Syria's Homs Press TV 27 Dec 2025 -- Hezbollah has strongly condemned a terrorist attack that targeted worshippers at a mosque in the western Syrian city of Homs during Friday prayers, leaving dozens of people killed and wounded.

  • Somalia officially demands Israel reverse recognition of Somaliland Press TV 27 Dec 2025 -- Somalia has formally demanded that the Israeli regime rescind its recognition of Somaliland, with Mogadishu describing the move as an unlawful violation of its sovereignty and territorial integrity.
  • Israel/Somalia: Statement by the Spokesperson on the territorial integrity of the Federal Republic of Somalia European External Action Service (EEAS) 27 Dec 2025 -- On 26 December, Israel recognised Somaliland as an independent state.
  • Iran condemns Israeli violation of Somalia's sovereignty and territorial integrity IRNA 27 Dec 2025 -- Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei has reaffirmed support for Somalia's sovereignty and territorial integrity, condemning an Israeli plot to break the East African nation by officially recognizing its self-declared region of Somaliland.
  • Iran says Israel's recognition of Somaliland aims to destabilize Red Sea, Horn of Africa Press TV 27 Dec 2025 -- Iran has strongly condemned the Israeli regime's blatant violation of Somalia's territorial integrity and national sovereignty by recognizing the self-declared Republic of Somaliland as a sovereign state.
  • Pakistan Rejects Attempts to Undermine Somalia's Sovereignty and Territorial Integrity Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 27 Dec 2025 -- Pakistan strongly condemns any attempts to undermine the sovereignty, unity, and territorial integrity of Somalia, and rejects, in this regard, the announcement made by Israel recognising the independence of the so-called Somaliland region of the Federal Republic of Somalia.
  • Saudi Arabia Affirms Support for Somalia's Sovereignty, Rejects Mutual Recognition between Israel and Somaliland SPA 27 Dec 2025 -- The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia affirmed full support for Somalia's sovereignty, unity, and territorial integrity, the Saudi Ministry of Foreign Affairs said in a statement

  • Ceasefire Between Cambodia and Thailand US Dept. of State 27 Dec 2025
  • Statement attributable to the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General - on Cambodia-Thailand UN Secretary-General 27 Dec 2025
  • Statement on Cambodia-Thailand ceasefire Australia DFAT 27 Dec 2025
  • Chinese foreign minister to meet with Cambodian, Thai counterparts in Yunnan following ceasefire agreement Global Times 27 Dec 2025 -- After a ceasefire agreement was reached by Cambodia and Thailand on Saturday, it was announced by a Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson that at the invitation of Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Minister of Foreign Affairs Wang Yi, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation of Cambodia Prak Sokhonn and Minister of Foreign Affairs of Thailand Sihasak Phuangketkeow will lead delegations to meet in Yunnan from Sunday to Monday.
  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson's Remarks on Cambodia and Thailand Signing a Joint Statement on Ceasefire PRC MofA 27 Dec 2025
  • The Ceasefire Agreement between the Kingdom of Cambodia and the Kingdom of Thailand (Statement by Press Secretary KITAMURA Toshihiro) Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 27 Dec 2025
  • DFA Statement on the Outcome of the 3rd Cambodia-Thailand GBC Philippines DFA 27 Dec 2025 -- The Philippines, as incoming ASEAN Chair, notes the successful convening of the 3rd Special General Border Committee (GBC) between Cambodia and Thailand, held at Pruk (Pailin Province of Cambodia) - Ban Park Krad (Chanthaburi Province of Thailand), where both countries released a Joint Statement renewing their ceasefire agreement.

  • UN renews ceasefire call as over 200 civilians killed in RSF ethnic attacks in Sudan's Darfur Press TV 27 Dec 2025 -- United Nations Secretary-General Antonio Guterres has called for an immediate ceasefire in Sudan following reports of ethnically motivated mass killings by the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) in Darfur.

  • Norway increases support for fight against hunger in South Sudan Government of Norway 27 Dec 2025 -- Norway is increasing support to the World Food Programme (WFP) in South Sudan. An additional USD Nok 128.6 million (approx. USD 12.7 million) will go towards the WFP's emergency assistance and efforts that strengthen community resilience.

News Reports

  • China launches Fengyun-4 03 satellite Global Times 27 Dec 2025 -- China on Saturday sent a new satellite into space from the Xichang Satellite Launch Center in the country's southwestern Sichuan Province.

  • Xi meeting would hinge on 1992 Consensus, anti-independence: KMT chair CNA 27 Dec 2025 -- Opposition Kuomintang (KMT) Chairwoman Cheng Li-wen (鄭麗文) said Saturday that any meeting she might have with Chinese President Xi Jinping (習近平) will come with no preconditions beyond adherence to the "1992 Consensus" and opposition to Taiwan independence.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 27 Dec 2025
  • Taiwan lawmakers vote to pass motion to impeach island's leader Lai Ching-te People's Daily 27 Dec 2025 -- An impeachment motion against Taiwan leader Lai Ching-te, submitted by lawmakers from the Chinese Kuomintang (KMT) party and Taiwan People's Party (TPP), was approved by the island's legislature on Friday.

  • Lecture Given to Mark 53rd Anniversary of Enactment of DPRK Constitution KCNA 27 Dec 2025 -- A lecture was given at the Central Youth Hall on Dec. 26 to mark the 53rd anniversary of the enactment of the DPRK Constitution.
  • North Korea, Russia bound in 'blood of war': Kim tells Putin in New Year note Press TV 27 Dec 2025 -- North Korean leader Kim Jong Un has said that ties between Pyongyang and Moscow have been sealed through "shared blood" shed during the Ukraine conflict, in a New Year message to Russian President Vladimir Putin.
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Sends Message of Greeting to Russian President KCNA 27 Dec 2025 -- Kim Jong Un , president of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, sent a message of greeting to Vladimir Vladimirovich Putin, president of the Russian Federation, on Dec. 27.

  • Commander: Defending homeland is common duty of all Iranians IRNA 27 Dec 2025 -- The Commander of the Islamic Republic of Iran Army Air Force Brigadier General Bahman Behmard says defending the homeland, family, and standing against enemy threats is a common duty for all Iranians, regardless of religion and denomination.
  • Iran's economy must not wait for sanctions relief: FM Araghchi IRNA 27 Dec 2025 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says Iran must continue its path of production, trade, and development without tying its future to the lifting of sanctions, stressing that diplomacy must pursue both sanctions relief and sanctions neutralization simultaneously.
  • Iran facing 'all-out war', stronger than before June war: President Press TV 27 Dec 2025 -- President Masoud Pezeshkian says the United States, Israel, and Europe are waging an "all-out war" against Iran, adding that the country is now stronger, in both military capability and manpower, than it was before the June military aggression.

  • Serbia's Prized Chinese Tire Factory Faces US Blockade Amid Forced Labor Concerns RFE/RL 27 Dec 2025 -- The United States has banned imports of car tires from the Chinese-owned Linglong factory in northern Serbia following years of allegations that workers there were subjected to forced labor and abusive conditions.
